description_long: This is a grand, ornate gate set within a stone and metal boundary structure, likely designed for a formal entrance such as a park, estate, or urban landmark. The central gate is flanked by two smaller pedestrian gates, all featuring intricate metalwork with decorative scrolls and finials. The main gate is supported by robust stone pillars topped with ornamental finials, and the adjoining sections consist of a combination of stone walls and black metal fencing. The metalwork is detailed and likely wrought iron, while the stonework appears to be finely cut and assembled. The overall design is symmetrical, with the central gate being the tallest and most prominent feature, and the decorative elements suggest a classical or baroque architectural influence.
description: A large, ornate metal and stone gate with decorative scrollwork, flanked by stone pillars and metal fencing. The design is symmetrical and highly detailed, suitable for a formal urban entrance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the gate, with the central ornate gate, flanking pedestrian gates, stone pillars, and metal fencing clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view showing the narrow profile of the gate, highlighting the thickness of the stone pillars and the depth of the metalwork.
description_view_2: Rear view, visually similar to the front, displaying the symmetrical structure and decorative elements from the opposite side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the narrow profile and the alignment of the stone and metal components.
